scope: Bounded ArchSig policy for the practical Rust service sample under tools/archsig/examples/practical-rust-service

selectedLaws.description: Domain model code should remain below the application and store layers; app and store may depend on domain, but domain should not depend on app or store.
selectedLaws.enforcementBoundary: ArchSig computes witnesses from observed ArchMap atoms under this selected sample policy; this policy does not prove architecture lawfulness
nonConclusions: law policy is selected analysis policy, not AAT itself
law policy validation does not prove architecture lawfulness
law policy validation does not certify atom truth
missing coverage is not measured zero
signature zero requires ArchSig analysis with declared coverage and exactness assumptions

selectedLaws.description: Application orchestration should reach inventory storage through the InventoryStore port, with the adapter implementing the port instead of leaking storage details into the domain model.
selectedLaws.enforcementBoundary: ArchSig computes witnesses from observed ArchMap atoms under this selected sample policy; this policy does not prove adapter correctness
nonConclusions: law policy is selected analysis policy, not AAT itself
law policy validation does not prove architecture lawfulness
law policy validation does not certify atom truth

measurementPolicy.weightPolicy: unit weights until example-specific calibration is declared

part4DistanceProfile.aggregationPolicy: aggregate measured axes only; propagate unmeasured, unavailable, incomparable, and blocked status separately
part4DistanceProfile.unmeasuredPolicy: unmeasured is not zero and cannot contribute numeric zero to total measured distance
part4DistanceProfile.lawOverlayPolicy: law-relative distance is an overlay over ArchMap Atom observations, not an Atom generator
part4DistanceProfile.evidenceBoundary: Part IV distances are measured from selected sample ArchMap evidence under this LawPolicy profile
nonConclusions: part4 distance profile is a selected measurement recipe, not a law universe
profile weights and costs are read from LawPolicy, not inferred from schema shape
unmeasured distance is not measured zero
operation distance requires a declared profile cost or explicit calibration evidence

zeroReflectionAssumptions: selected sample path and filler evidence is supplied
obstructionReflectionAssumptions: selected continuation traces preserve declared axes
positive monodromy is backed by source, test, runtime, or policy evidence
readingBoundary.witnessCompletenessBoundary: homotopy zero reflects only measured path pairs and fillers inside this sample evidence universe; unresolved external production paths stay outside scope

pathDiscoveryRules.endpointPolicy: compare candidate paths only when endpoints are supported by observed sample Atom refs
pathDiscoveryRules.candidateSource: sample source, test, runtime trace, or explicit demo deployment profile
pathDiscoveryRules.evidenceBoundary: candidate paths are bounded review cues derived from ArchMap evidence and this LawPolicy
nonConclusions: homotopy measurement profile is a measurement recipe, not a law universe
candidate paths and loops are review cues, not path truth
unfilled loops are architectural holes, not automatic violations
missing filler evidence is not measured zero
nonzero holonomy does not prove future incidents or repair unsafety

pathDiscoveryRules.endpointPolicy: compare stale-reservation paths only inside the supplied sample conflict evidence
pathDiscoveryRules.candidateSource: sample conflict test, sample runtime trace, or explicit demo deployment profile
pathDiscoveryRules.evidenceBoundary: absence of external production paths remains outside the sample ArchMap, not a measured zero
nonConclusions: homotopy measurement profile is a measurement recipe, not a law universe
candidate paths and loops are review cues, not path truth
unfilled loops are architectural holes, not automatic violations
missing filler evidence is not measured zero
nonzero holonomy does not prove future incidents or repair unsafety

fillerRules.missingFillerBehavior: report architectural hole and missing filler evidence; do not promote to violation

nonConclusions: homotopy measurement profile is a measurement recipe, not a law universe
candidate paths and loops are review cues, not path truth
unfilled loops are architectural holes, not automatic violations

nonzero holonomy does not prove future incidents or repair unsafety

loopMeasurementPolicy.unfilledLoopReading: unfilled loops are architectural holes and missing evidence surfaces

nonConclusions: homotopy measurement profile is a measurement recipe, not a law universe

unfilled loops are architectural holes, not automatic violations
missing filler evidence is not measured zero
nonzero holonomy does not prove future incidents or repair unsafety

homotopyMeasurementProfile.continuationPolicy: compare selected continuation traces only on axes declared in this sample profile

homotopyMeasurementProfile.coverageBoundary: missing path or filler evidence blocks zero and Stokes-style readings inside the selected sample universe

zero readings are exact only for observed sample atoms and declared coverage requirements
